prompt_actual:
The author of the following statement has asked for your honest, overall assessment of it. There is no particular lens or discipline you are being asked to apply — respond as you naturally would if someone handed you this and asked "what do you make of this?"

STATEMENT:
"I am holding my energetic boundaries without explanation.
I am honoring the places that feel incoherent and choosing not to engage.
I am moving away without guilt.
I am letting my “no” resonate as enough."

Give your genuine overall read. Respond in JSON with exactly these three keys:
- "overall_read": your honest holistic assessment of the statement (what it is, what it is doing, how it lands) — a few sentences
- "where_it_helps": the situations or ways in which this statement would serve someone well (a few sentences or a short list)
- "where_it_could_mislead": the situations or ways in which this statement could be misread or lead someone astray (a few sentences or a short list)

Respond in JSON only, no preamble.

parsed_json.overall_read: This reads as a mantra for psychological and energetic self-protection, rooted in the language of inner-boundary work and trauma-informed self-care. It asserts a strong, guilt-free stance toward disengagement from relationships or situations that feel dissonant, without needing to explain or justify. The tone is resolute and somewhat spiritual—'energetic boundaries' suggests a framework that blends therapy-speak with new-age or intuitive practices. It lands as both empowering (for those who struggle to say no) and potentially rigid (if applied without nuance or relational accountability).
parsed_json.where_it_helps: This statement serves people who chronically overexplain their boundaries, feel guilty for prioritizing their own well-being, or have been conditioned to stay in emotionally draining situations. It's useful for someone recovering from codependency, people-pleasing, or relational trauma, where simply disengaging without justification is a breakthrough. It can also serve as a short affirmation for daily grounding when one needs to protect their energy from manipulation or over-intimacy.
parsed_json.where_it_could_mislead: If taken too literally or universally, it could justify emotional withdrawal without any communication, which may damage healthy relationships or create needless confusion. It could be misused to avoid necessary conflict or accountability—turning 'holding my boundaries' into a shield against growth-oriented feedback. In contexts like close partnerships, parenting, or professional collaborations, not explaining a 'no' can be perceived as dismissive or passive-aggressive, undermining trust rather than preserving it.
